REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT SONY CORPORATION OF AMERICA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / SUMMARY ADJUDICATION: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT EXIST REGARDING WAIVER OF TIMELY BILLING PROVISIONS OF THE LEASE. SEE ADDITIONAL FACTS 34, 35 AND 36. DEFENDANTS ALSO DISPUTE PLAINTIFF'S ADDITIONAL FACT 26 AND PRESENT EVIDENCE IN REPLY TO SUPPORT THIS DISPUTED FACT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. CCP 437C(F)(1). NONE OF THE PURPORTED ISSUES FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION "COMPLETELY DISPOSED OF A CAUSE OF ACTION, AND AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE, A CLAIM FOR DAMAGES, OR AN ISSUE OF DUTY." MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS FRAMED BY THE PLEADINGS WHICH ASSERT THAT "SONY HAS FAILED TO PAY ADDITIONAL CHARGES INCURRED FOR THE YEARS 2018 THROUGH 2021 THE PAST DUE AMOUNT OWED TO PLAINTIFF IS $442,950.82, PLUS ACCRUED INTEREST." (COMPLAINT, PARAGRAPH 8). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-09-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Phillip F. Shinn; LimNexus LLP; 213-955-9500; appearing as counsel for Defendant Sony Interactive Entertainment LLC.
Jessica M. Takano; Donahue Fitzgerald LLP; 510-451-3300; appearing as counsel for Plaintiff

Defendant contests the court's tentative decision. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SONY CORPORATION OF AMERICA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / SUMMARY ADJUDICATION: Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Triable issues of fact exist regarding waiver of timely billing provisions of the lease. See additional facts 34, 35 and 36. Defendants also dispute Plaintiff's additional fact 26 and present evidence in reply to support this disputed fact.

Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. CCP 437c(f)(1). None of the purported issues for summary adjudication "completely disposed of a cause of action, and affirmative defense, a claim for damages, or an issue of duty." Motion for Summary Adjudication is framed by the pleadings which assert that "Sony has failed to pay Additional Charges incurred for the years 2018 through 2021 the past due amount owed to Plaintiff is $442,950.82, plus accrued interest." (Complaint, paragraph 8).

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

The parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the judge pro tem. The parties and judge pro tem (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the stipulation on their behalf.

Off Calendar Jul-05-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT SONY CORPORATION OF AMERICA's Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Jessica M. Takano, Esq., of DONAHUE FITZGERALD LLP, (510) 451-3300, for Plaintiff.
Phillip F. Shinn, Esq., of LIMNEXUS LLP, (213) 955-9500, for Defendant.

The parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the judge pro tem. The parties and judge pro tem (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the stipulation on their behalf.

The matter is argued. At conclusion of argument, the matter is taken under submission. Counsel for Plaintiff and Defendant are instructed to each submit a proposed order for the judge pro tem's review via email to psw@hassard.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)
